body.title: Versioned memories — version control for memory nodes
body.result: Child-chain version history: version_before_update() snapshots current state as child. Wired into cortex.store(). get_version_history() for querying. 7 tests.
body.description:
Add version control to memory nodes. Each edit creates a new version; previous versions are preserved and queryable. Like git for individual memories.

## Motivation
Memories evolve — facts get corrected, interpretations deepen, procedures improve. Currently edits overwrite in place, losing history. Versioning preserves the evolution and enables rollback.

## Examples
- IDENTITY.md is versioned via github — but the graph-native equivalent has no history
- A FACTUAL memory about a tool's API gets updated when the API changes — old version is useful context
- A PROCEDURAL habit gets refined through experimentation — seeing the evolution helps understand why

## Open questions (explore with Igor)
- Does Igor find this useful? Would he use version history in reasoning?
- Storage: versions as child nodes? Separate versions table? JSONB array in metadata?
- Granularity: every edit, or only significant changes?
- Retention: keep all versions forever, or prune old versions of low-value memories?

## Depends on
- Nothing specific — but benefits from the three-schema architecture (clan schema)
